name: registry-approval
description: Shared registry with explicit proposal and approval steps before records become active or revoked.
use_case: Use Xian as the authoritative shared approval and registry layer while Python services and workers hydrate richer proposal and record views for operators and applications.

starter_flows.name: local
starter_flows.summary: Run the approval workflow locally with indexed proposal history, projection hydration, and the deeper API/projector flow.

steps.title: Create and start the local indexed node
commands: uv run xian network create registry-approval-local --template single-node-indexed --chain-id registry-approval-local-1 --generate-validator-key --init-node
uv run xian node start validator-1
uv run xian node health validator-1
notes: Run these commands from the xian-cli checkout.
The local indexed template gives you BDS reads and dashboard/monitoring defaults for proposal workflows.

steps.title: Bootstrap the registry and approval contracts
notes: Run these commands from the xian-py checkout.
Set XIAN_NODE_URL, XIAN_CHAIN_ID, XIAN_WALLET_PRIVATE_KEY, and any extra signer env vars before bootstrapping.

steps.title: Run the projector and API
notes: The projector uses indexed events as triggers and authoritative contract reads to hydrate proposal and record state.
The API exposes projected pending-approval views alongside direct contract reads.

starter_flows.name: remote
starter_flows.summary: Deploy the pack on a small shared-state network with xian-deploy and use the same proposal, approval, and projection pattern remotely.

steps.title: Create the shared-network bundle
commands: uv run xian network create registry-approval-remote --template consortium-3 --chain-id registry-approval-remote-1 --generate-validator-key
notes: Run this command from the xian-cli checkout.
The consortium template is the intended operator posture for this shared approval use case.

steps.title: Deploy validators and the service node
notes: Run these commands from the xian-deploy checkout with your inventory for the validator and service-node hosts.
Use the health playbook to validate BDS, monitoring, and state-sync readiness after deployment.

steps.title: Bootstrap contracts and application-side services
notes: Run these commands from the xian-py checkout against the remote RPC/BDS endpoints.
Configure additional signers and signer funding in the admin job env before running the approval flow.
